#### Description

When trying to compile templates, the compilation process fails with a `TypeError: Can't compile non template nodes` error. This appears to affect basic template operations and rendering.

#### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
from jinja2 import Environment, DictLoader

# Basic template compilation
env = Environment(loader=DictLoader({'test.html': 'Hello {{ name }}!'}))
template = env.get_template('test.html')
result = template.render(name='World')
print(result)
```

The above code should render "Hello World!" but instead raises:
```
TypeError: Can't compile non template nodes
```

This also affects more complex template operations:

```python
from jinja2 import Environment

env = Environment()
tmpl = env.from_string('{{ -1|abs }}|{{ 1|abs }}')
result = tmpl.render()
print(result)  # Should print "1|1"
```

#### Expected Behavior

Templates should compile and render normally without throwing compilation errors.

#### Actual Behavior

Template compilation fails with `TypeError: Can't compile non template nodes` preventing any template rendering from working.

Reasoning: generate() currently returns an empty string when no stream is passed, so Environment.compile ends up compiling an empty source. That produces a code object that doesn't populate the template namespace (missing "name"), causing KeyError: 'name'. The correct behavior is to return the generated source when stream is None (i.e. caller expects the source returned), and return None when a stream was passed (caller wrote into provided stream). I'll make a small targeted fix in src/jinja2/compiler.py to swap that logic.

```bash

old = (
    "    generator.visit(node)\n\n"
    "    if stream is not None:\n"
    "        return generator.stream.getvalue()  # type: ignore\n\n"
    "    return \"\"\n"
)
if old in s:
    new = (
        "    generator.visit(node)\n\n"
        "    # If no stream was provided, return the generated source. If a\n"
        "    # stream was provided the generator wrote to that stream and we\n"
        "    # should return None.\n"
        "    if stream is None:\n"
        "        return generator.stream.getvalue()  # type: ignore\n\n"
        "    return None\n"
    )
    s = s.replace(old, new, 1)
